titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Kate Mooney, December 30th 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Peter Mooney (b.1886) to his sister, Katie (b.1884). Peter complains that the puttees and breeches sent by Katie are of the wrong colour - they must be khaki. Peter writes of the loneliness of conducting picket duty, guarding the horses at night but writes of having an unusual companion during his duty in the form of a little dog.Orphaned at age 7, Peter and his sister Katie were raised by their uncle William and his wife Mary in Caulstown. While there are no surviving enlistment papers for Peter, his War Badge Index Record shows his enlistment date as 18 October 1915. He served with the Royal Artillery and later transferred to the Royal Engineers where he was promoted to the rank of Sergeant. He served in several places at the Western Front, including Ypres, St. Omer and Passchendael. Peter and Kate were avid correspondents. This letter part of a collection of over ninety surviving letters.

msIdentifier.collection: Mooney Family Papers, Mooney Family Papers

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Nov 30th1915 Dear Katie. I received the Breeches
& Puttees alright this morning.
Unfortunately they are the wrong
ones. You know the riding
Breeches with no strapping
they are partly a Khaki
colour. The Puttees will
hardly do you can get a
good thick pair in Dublin
You can get them where you
got the Numerals & send them
on. It will send back the
others when I get them
The sort of thing Picquet
Duty is as follows. We go
on Duty at 4.30 until 6.06
next morning. We have to be
on the watch two hours at
a time minding the Horses
The night I was on duty I
was out from 6.06 until 8.06
&then back again from 12.06 until 2.06
We have to see that nothing
happens the Horses during the
night. It is a lonesome
job. I am glad poor old
Barney is alive still I hope
he lives until I see him
again. I got a letter from Chris
Meehan this morning which
I enclose. He is going on
well thank God. I have
found out the name of the
Col. It is John Harvey
He must be an Irishman
by his name. You can
show Chris Meehans letter
to his mother. John Meehan
is in England still. The
little dog was with me
the night I was on Piquit
Duty he is a nice little fellow
I suppose Paddy goes up
ofter to you I hope he does
